goldfish_fact: Contrary to popular belief, goldfish have a memory span of several months, not 3 seconds.
query: What is the actual memory span of goldfish?
distractors: The Great Wall of China stretches over 13,000 miles and was built across multiple dynasties, primarily during the Ming period.
Photosynthesis converts sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water into glucose and oxygen, powering nearly all plant life on Earth.
The Eiffel Tower was completed in 1889 as the entrance to the World Fair and stands at 330 meters including antennas.
Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found pots of honey in ancient Egyptian tombs that are over 3,000 years old and still edible.
The human body contains about 37 trillion cells, each playing a specialized role in keeping us alive.
Mount Everest grows approximately 4 millimeters taller each year due to tectonic plate movement in the Himalayas.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood because their circulatory system uses copper-based hemocyanin instead of iron-based hemoglobin.
The Amazon rainforest produces about 20 percent of the world's oxygen and is home to over 400 billion individual trees.
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth, traveling at roughly 300,000 kilometers per second.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean on Earth, covering more than 63 million square miles and reaching depths of nearly 11 kilometers in the Mariana Trench.
Bees communicate the location of flowers to their hive mates through a dance known as the waggle dance, first decoded by Karl von Frisch.
The speed of sound in dry air at 20 degrees Celsius is approximately 343 meters per second, though it varies with temperature and humidity.
Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system with surface temperatures around 465 degrees Celsius, hotter than Mercury despite being farther from the Sun.
